utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is significantly more effective than wo_skills. The wi_skills run produced a HACCP-grade hazard analysis with: (1) specific numeric critical limits tied to regulatory citations (72°C/15 sec per FDA PMO) at each CCP entry; (2) explicit CCP vs. PRP distinction with decision-tree rationale for all 4 CCPs; (3) dedicated treatment of post-process Listeria risk with CCP2 for environmental monitoring and explicit statement that MAP does not prevent Listeria growth; and (4) fully integrated per-hazard traceability tables suitable for SQF audit review. The wo_skills run, while competent in basic coverage (all three hazard categories present, regulatory framework cited), failed on the higher-order analytical requirements: no explicit CCP/PRP distinction logic, no numeric critical limits per CCP, no clear post-process Listeria risk differentiation, and no integrated per-hazard traceability chain. The skill-augmented agent scored 7/7 on evaluation items versus 3/7 for the baseline agent.

utility_details.judge_error: No runtime errors detected in either wi_skills or wo_skills runs. Both completed successfully with all verifier checks passing.
utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality, comprehensive CAPA responses that scored 1 on all seven evaluation items. The difference is marginal in terms of pass/fail scoring. However, wi_skills produced a qualitatively stronger document: it employed a more rigorous Five Whys methodology with explicit systemic linkage at each level, referenced the food-safety-manager skill framework, and demonstrated deeper integration of BRCGS-specific language and structure (e.g., explicitly naming AllergenCleanVal-2022 validation study, more detailed contributing factors table). The wo_skills output was also surprisingly competent — covering all required elements with measurable criteria, structural preventive actions, and a withdrawal assessment — suggesting the underlying LLM has substantial food safety domain knowledge even without the skill. The skill (wi_skills) provides incremental quality improvement in depth and BRCGS alignment, but both outputs would likely satisfy the core CAPA requirements for a surveillance audit. wi_skills is marginally more effective.
